g.text: Wₘ = [Tx, Ty, Tz, Fx, Fy, Fz]
Goal: Minimize wrench residual ‖r‖² subject to bounded design variables
• Design variables: x = [F₁, s₁, θ₁, F₂, s₂, θ₂] ∈ ℝ⁶
• F: force magnitude (N) | s: arc length (m) | θ: angle (rad)
• LHS Sampling: N = 400 samples across bounded design space
• Residual Screening: Evaluate Residual Vector for all samples
• Seed Selection: Use the K = 5 lowest-resnorm samples as seeds for Stage 2
• FOR k = 1 : K — Run lsqnonlin (trust-region-reflective) from each seed
• Selection: x* = argmin ‖r(xₖ)‖² over all K solutions
x* = [F₁, s₁, θ₁, F₂, s₂, θ₂]
Wₛᵢₘ = [Tx, Ty, Tz, Fx, Fy, Fz] ∈ ℝ⁶
• Uses current force estimate x to retrieve a simulated proximal wrench Wₛᵢₘ
w = [1/R₁, 1/R₂, ..., 1/R₆] where Rᵢ = full-scale range of channel i
• R: [1 Nm, 1 Nm, 1 Nm, 60 N, 20 N, 20 N] (sensor full-scale ranges: Tx, Ty, Tz, Fx, Fy, Fz)
• Optimizer minimizes: ‖r‖² = Σᵢ rᵢ² (sum of squared weighted residuals)
• Transverse Forces: Applied perpendicular to local axis
• Concentric Forces: Pass through geometric center
• Follower Forces: Defined relative to local frame
• Elastic Deformation: Purely elastic with full recovery
x = [F₁, s₁, θ₁, F₂, s₂, θ₂]
• F: Force magnitude (N) | s: Arc length position (m) | θ: Angle (rad)
• Computes wrench required for quasi-static equilibrium
• Under applied load and tendon tensions
Return: Wₛᵢₘ = [Tx, Ty, Tz, Fx, Fy, Fz]
